items.title: Modular layouts solve awkward yard geometry
items.quote: Public review synthesis: shoppers value that one kit can adapt to narrow, angled, L-shaped, U-shaped, or compact spaces without custom carpentry.
items.recommendedAction: Add a layout finder that starts from yard shape and recommends kit configuration, height, and accessories.

items.title: Higher price needs stronger long-term value proof
items.quote: Public review synthesis: the main purchase hesitation is price versus lower-cost galvanized beds, even when shoppers like the durability story.
items.recommendedAction: Add a cost-per-season comparison module and make the 20-year durability claim easier to evaluate.

items.title: Protective film removal extends assembly time
items.quote: Public hands-on testing noted that setup was straightforward, but removing protective film from panels created unexpected extra work.
items.recommendedAction: Add a pre-assembly tip card and short video specifically about protective film removal.

items.title: Actual assembly time can exceed expectations
items.quote: Public tester feedback indicates assembly can take much longer than the fastest advertised expectation, even when the build is manageable.
items.recommendedAction: Show realistic assembly time ranges by model and number of people instead of a single best-case estimate.

items.title: Material safety and rust resistance drive trust
items.quote: Public buyer-guide coverage repeatedly highlights corrosion resistance, food-safe coating, and long service life as core reasons to choose Vego.
items.recommendedAction: Create a durability proof section with coating explanation, food safety, corrosion testing, and expected lifespan.

items.title: Small-animal protection may require add-ons
items.quote: Public buying advice suggests some gardens may still need mesh or short screens to keep smaller animals out.
items.recommendedAction: Bundle pest-screen recommendations by region, crop, and bed height.

items.title: Elevated height is a clear accessibility benefit
items.quote: Hands-on coverage emphasizes that elevated beds make plant inspection and maintenance easier for users avoiding bending or crouching.
items.recommendedAction: Segment elevated beds for accessibility, back comfort, patios, and older gardeners.

items.title: Elevated beds need clearer soil quantity guidance
items.quote: Public review coverage calls out that elevated beds use less soil than larger in-ground beds, making capacity and bag estimates important.
items.recommendedAction: Add a soil bag calculator to each PDP, including Hugelkultur-style fill guidance where appropriate.

items.title: Compact shipping can create missing-parts anxiety
items.quote: Hands-on coverage noted the box was surprisingly compact, which created initial concern before confirming all parts were included.
items.recommendedAction: Add an unboxing checklist and packing diagram to reduce setup anxiety.

items.title: Trellis arches expand small-space gardening appeal
items.quote: Public coverage frames trellis arches as a way to add vertical growing capacity and visual impact without expanding the garden footprint.
items.recommendedAction: Cross-sell trellis arches on small-space and tomato/cucumber bed pages.

items.title: Trellis setup is praised when quick and sturdy
items.quote: Public shopper feedback summarized by media highlights easy trellis installation and a sturdy feel as key confidence drivers.
items.recommendedAction: Use short installation clips and sturdiness proof points in trellis PDP media.

items.title: Crop type should guide bed depth selection
items.quote: Public buyer guidance notes that herbs and flowers can use shallower beds, while deep-rooted crops need more soil depth.
items.recommendedAction: Add a crop-to-depth selector for herbs, tomatoes, cucumbers, eggplants, and root vegetables.
